name: Jones v. Hendrix
summary: Held that a prisoner who had already filed a Section 2255 motion could not use Section 2241 to raise a statutory claim that his conduct was not criminal, because Congress specifically limited second or successive 2255 motions to constitutional claims.
significance: The government cited it to show that Congress deliberately limited when prisoners can bring second or successive habeas claims, allowing only constitutional (not statutory) claims. The petitioner cited it to distinguish the kind of statutory conflict that triggers implicit preclusion.
